verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (UDP-Gal:betaGlcNAc beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a Golgi-resident glycosyltransferase with a single transmembrane domain anchoring it to the Golgi membrane. The TM domain is positioned to place the catalytic domain in the Golgi lumen. However, B4GALT1 is well-documented to have a surface pool on the plasma membrane in addition to its dominant Golgi localization. Surface expression of B4GALT1 has been demonstrated by cell-surface biotinylation and flow cytometry in multiple cell types. The protein cycles between the Golgi and PM via secretory trafficking pathways, maintaining a stable minority surface pool. This PM residency is constitutive (not induced) and reflects its own TM anchor and participation in normal secretory cycling. The surface-exposed catalytic domain can mediate galactosylation of cell-surface glycoproteins and glycolipids, making it functionally relevant at the cell surface.
